title: SACRAMENTO, Calif. — Assemblymember Tasha Boerner Horvath’s (D-Encinitas) bill to create the position of Deputy Secretary for Climate was signed by Governor Newsom on Friday.
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2204 aims to establish the position of Deputy Secretary for Climate within California’s Labor and Workforce Development Agency.",
    "The Deputy Secretary for Climate will assist with the oversight of California's transition to a sustainable and equitable carbon neutral economy.",
    "California is moving away from fossil fuels toward renewables to meet greenhouse gas emission reduction targets.",
    "The Deputy Secretary for Climate will ensure California has the jobs and trained workforce necessary to support its carbon-neutral energy needs.",
    "In 2019, one out of six clean energy jobs in America was located in California.",
    "A report states there are about 113,000 people employed in fossil fuel and ancillary industries in California.",
    "A 50% closure of the oil and gas industry from 2021 to 2030 would result in approximately 57,600 job losses statewide over 10 years.",
    "One major hurdle in transitioning from fossil fuel to clean energy is anticipating and mitigating the economic impact on workers and communities invested in those industries.",
    "The Deputy Secretary for Climate is tasked with monitoring and coordinating California's progress toward 100% clean energy.",
    "The Deputy Secretary for Climate will identify the timing and location of fossil fuel facility closures and related workforce transition needs."
  ]
}
```
